Problem

as a conscious &

The problem:
Year
2017
When Kabir entered the stagnant mattress industry, he saw a major gap: the lengthy chain of manufacturing to consumer delivery—distribution, logistics, warehousing, and retail—unnecessarily inflated prices. Worse, the quality of these overpriced mattresses didn’t justify the cost. He set out to simplify this
The Answer: 
Pioneer the concept of delivering mattresses in a box in India at direct-from-factory costs.
This meant we had full control on Quality & no middle-men or distributors in the picture.
SleepyCat was born
Why the name? 

A famous animated character back in the 90’s called Garfield was the inspiration to the name 

This beloved Orange cat..
Hated Monday 
Loved Lasagna  
Loved to Sleep 

Rebrand 2020
As we grew, we realized comfort is emotional & aspirational, not just functional. 
This sparked our rebrand:


Year
2020
Fresh
Visuals
Evolved
Tone
Customer
Focus
It was then when we set out to celebrates rest, making it irresistible. We’re on a mission to normalize guilt-free relaxation, creating products people love to help them invest in their rest—because, truly, sleep solves everything.
Vibrant colors & playful designs celebrating relaxation.AKA the art of being chill!
Conversational, witty, & comforting.
Real stories & benefits to connect meaningfully with our audience.

Sleepycat : Identity Design
September 2021

Sleepycat X Thought Over Design

Our product is better because we offer more at much less.. 
Buy it!

Our product is better because we offer more at much less.. 
Buy it!


We encourage prioritizing rest
with products the help you rest better.
Try us
Why We Exist
We exist because we believe everyone deserves unparalleled comfort. By reimagining rest, we’re changing how people experience their daily lives. Our vision? To be the go-to brand for comfort, inside and out.

Mission: To help people heal through rest and experience joy in comfort.

Vision: To make the world a chilled out place, one amazingly comfortable product at a time.

SLEEPYCAT VS. OTHERS in 2025
Sleepycat X Thought Over Design
Our Foundation Pillars - PPP
People:We’re busupporting a team of skilled, passionate individuals who believe in delivering premium experiences. We need each other and our shared beliefs to succeed.
Product:By designing with purpose, we break barriers and set new standards in comfort. Our products aren’t just functional; they win hearts.
Process:Every action we take affects another. Clear communication and adherence to processes ensure productivity and growth for all.
Core Values:
Our work at SleepyCat is guided by six core values that define our attitudes and beliefs:
Simplicity: From our products to our processes, we keep things simple to enhance well-being.
Customer Obsession: We exist to make every moment more comfortable for our customers, inside and outside the company.
Humility: We value every voice, stay open to learning, and always strive to improve.
Ownership & Bias for Action: We solve problems proactively, take charge, and stay accountable for results—no excuses!
Passion & Excellence: We bring passion to everything we do and aim for excellence in even the smallest details.
Empathy & Entrepreneurship: We care deeply for our customers and each other, acting with ownership, innovation, and understanding.
Brand personality & Comms
Meet our Mascot

Sleepycat X Thought Over Design
Our Brand Personality
We’re conversational, comforting, and witty (with a side of sass)We bring a sense of relaxation and joy to everything we do. Our tone of voice and communication style reflect this, ensuring every interaction reinforces our identity. 
Approachable and Human: Speak like a friend, not a corporation. Use language that is easy to understand and relatable.Example: "Need help picking the perfect mattress? We’re here for you!" vs. "For assistance, please consult our mattress guide."
Witty and Fun: Add a touch of humor and playfulness where appropriate, making interactions enjoyable.Example: "The comfiest bed you’ll never want to leave… unless there’s coffee!" vs. "Our bed is comfortable and functional."
Empathetic and Supportive: Show understanding and care, especially when addressing customer concerns.Example: "We’re sorry you’re not happy. Let’s make it right!" vs. "Your complaint has been noted."
Use a friendly, conversational tone. Keep it light and personal.
Sound overly formal or robotic.
Incorporate wit and positivity where appropriate.
Use humor in serious or sensitive situations.
DOs
DON’Ts
Talking to customers:
Always prioritize clarity, warmth, and authenticity. 
Represent SleepyCat as a brand that genuinely cares about their comfort and happiness.
Be humble in your interactions and be open to understand a customer’s perspective

Brief Doc_ Brand Truth Sleepycat.docx:
INTERNAL BRAND POSITIONING & EXECUTION BRIEF

Our core problem, today. 

Our growth is subjective to existing intent, not creating demand.

Performance marketing controls budgets through ROAS

Brand has limited control over what is being said or why

Scaling spend breaks unit economics

Creative fatigue sets in quickly

Growth plateaus because demand itself is insufficient

"Pure brand/ awareness" work without accountability is not acceptable

The loop we have always been stuck in:


Weak demand → performance optimises harder → ROAS breaks → spend pulled back → growth stalls



What marketing really needs to do for Sleepycat: 



Make Sleepycat instantly relatable - not just known or visible and,

SleepyCat is not here to be:

The viral social media brand

The funniest mattress brand

The preachy brand on social media 

The loudest brand on social

To really stand out in terms of relevance and recall, SleepyCat must become the brand people understand.

If people don’t understand what problem we solve, no amount of media spend scales.

2. THE CORE BELIEF

Internal belief (what we know from now)

Sleep has a job: to reset your body overnight.
If you wake up worse than when you went to bed, sleep didn’t do its job.

SleepyCat helps people understand when sleep is failing and how the right mattress can help their body wake up better.

Understand this as “recovery” in terms of internal language but we will not market ourselves as a "recovery brand" or a fitness-only brand.





External framing

Consumers don’t think about “recovery”.
They think about how they wake up.

If you wake up worse than when you went to bed, sleep didn’t do its job.

Everything we say flows from this.

So our language is not:

Recovery brand

Medical brand

Fitness sleep

Optimisation sleep

Long sleep vs short sleep

Our language is:

Morning pain

Morning stiffness

Waking up worse

Waking up feeling physically fatigued

3. Phase 1: We go narrow with one understandable entry signal and purpose to busupport

Primary entry point:
Morning pain & stiffness after sleep

Woke up with back pain?

Body tightness

“Subah uthte hi dard / stiffness”

Why this signal

High lived relevance

Self-diagnosable

Emotionally sharp

Forces people to rethink sleep quality

What we are NOT starting with

Generic tiredness or generic pain 

Morning mood 

Sleep hours

“Better sleep”

Optimisation language

Those come later after belief is built.



4. What we definitely own (and dont)

Unlike other brands, We do NOT own symptoms

We are not:

“Back pain mattress”

“Orthopedic brand”

“Fixes pain”

medicinal

Why is this distinction very important?

If we let "ortho mattress" become our identity:

We commoditise ourselves

We invite feature comparisons

We narrow future growth narratives

We lose premium & aspiration

We own the space of understanding the diagnosis, not the science or symptoms. 

If pain or stiffness is worse in the morning, something likely went wrong during sleep.

We explain the meaning of the symptom not claim to cure it. We explain how relief can happen, not promise it.

This gives us:

Authority without medical claims

A framework to sell many products

Protection from becoming an ortho-only brand

5. Possibility of relief and recovery: Our exact position

SleepyCat designs sleep surfaces that enable better recovery. Back pain is one of the most common symptoms of poor recovery.

Back pain is:

A symptom people already recognise

A clear entry point to talk about sleep quality

A way to reframe recovery, not define the brand

This allows us to later expand naturally into:

Soft<> Firm and importance of, and what it means for your mornings

Deep sleep

Performance

Premium recovery (Founder's, etc.)



We sell the possibility of relief and recovery, not guarantees, not claims.

Exactly like Sensodyne:

Explain mechanism or the ‘need’ for something specific

Avoid promises

Busupport trust

Sensodyne's marketing success stems from its focus on educating consumers about tooth sensitivity, leveraging dentist endorsements, maintaining consistent messaging about relief, and a "think global, advertise local" approach with premium positioning, making it a leader by busupporting trust and addressing an unmet need for pain relief in hot/cold foods

Language that allows this:

“Designed to reduce pressure on specific body points”

“Helps your body relax fully during sleep”

Designed to give you the right balance, the kind that supports you.

“Supports better overnight recovery”

“Many people feel less stiffness over time”

Not allowed

“Fixes back pain”

“Guaranteed relief”

“Cures”

“Doctor recommended”

We explain how relief can happen — never promise it.

Primary audiences identified

A. Athletes & Serious Fitness Users

Runners, crossfitters, gym-goers, yoga practitioners

Believe pain is due to training load or form

Already stretch, strengthen, recover — yet pain persists

Core tension:

"I do everything right — why does my back still hurt?"

B. Desk Professionals with Active Lifestyles

Corporate jobs + workouts

Posture-aware, physio-aware

Still wake up stiff

Core tension:

"I train and stretch, but mornings feel broken."

C. Performance-Oriented Professionals (30–45) 

Busy, high stress, limited recovery windows

Value optimisation and efficiency

Open to premium if logic is sound

Core tension:

"I don't have time to recover properly."

WHAT DOES THIS MEAN FOR OUR COMMUNICATION FUNNELS?

#1 TOFU:BELIEF & SELF-RECOGNITION:

Job: “This is me.”

What market must do at this stage:

Trigger recognition

Create emotional clarity

Make people feel seen

NOT


NOT sell

NOT explain product

NOT push features


What does copy sound like at the top?

Sounds like

Situational

Observational

First-person or second-person

No brand hype

“Ever notice your back hurts more after sleeping than before?”

“Sleeping 8 hours but waking up stiff feels illegal.”

“The worst part of back pain is realizing sleep didn’t fix it.”


Doesnt say:

Buy now

Orthopedic

Memory foam

Discount

Doctor recommended

No product selling.
Brand is present, not dominant. Subtle integration but nothing loud. 

Success metrics:

Saves

Shares

Watch time

Comment language echo

Not judged on:

ROAS

CAC

#2 MOFU: EDUCATION & REFRAMING

Job: Change causality in the mind.
 From: “I have back pain”
 To: “Maybe my sleep is failing me.”

Where does MOFU thrive?

Retargeting ads (most important)

Education pages , SEO Pages, Comparison pages (not PDPs)

Belief sections inside PDPs

30–45 sec explainer videos

SEO problem pages

Decision guides

FAQ hubs

Creator follow-up content

Retail scripts (first 2–3 mins) / CE scripts

"Why recovery happens during sleep, not training" "Why firm mattresses fail athletes over time" "Pressure relief vs firmness; what your spine needs"

Sounds like

Calm

Explanatory

Credible

No hype

No medical claims

Examples

"During deep sleep, muscles are supposed to relax completely. If pressure points stay active all night, your body never fully recovers — no matter how well you train."

“If pain is worse in the morning, it often means your body didn’t relax properly during sleep.”

“Sleep isn’t just rest. It’s when your body resets. If you wake up stiff, that reset may not be happening.”

“Many people chase pain relief, but the real question is: what’s happening overnight?”

Language to use

“Designed to help…”

“Supports…”

“Many people feel…”

“Over time…”

Still NOT allowed

“Fixes pain”

“Guaranteed relief”

“Cures”

MOFU is evaluated on

Watch time (if its a video)

Completion rate

Time on page

Scroll depth

CTR to PDP

ROAS here is a guardrail, not a success metric.

#3 BOFU: CONVERSION & ROUTING

Job: Help people choose confidently. Now we can finally sell. 

“Who this product is exactly for / not for”

Product explainers

Testimonials framed as before vs after waking state : again no guarantees

"What makes a mattress genuinely good for muscle recovery"

"Why hybrid latex supports your back"

"Why SleepyCat designs for recovery, not firmness"

Where?

Short proof ads

PDP modules aligned to belief

Realistic testimonials

FAQ-driven reassurance

Sounds like

Clear

Specific

Confident

Product-forward

Examples

"I didn't stop training. I didn't change workouts. I just fixed the surface I recover on, and mornings changed."

“Designed for people who wake up with back stiffness.”

“If mornings feel worse than nights, this mattress is built for you.”

“Pressure-relieving layers that help your body relax overnight.”

“Not for: people who like ultra-soft sink-in beds.”

Allowed now

Features

Materials

Proof points

Reviews

Pricing cues

Visuals

Product clearly shown

Layer breakdown

Comparison cards

Testimonials

Badges (trial, warranty, etc.)

CTA

“Find your fit.”

“Choose your comfort.”

“Shop the mattress for better mornings.”

This lane is judged hard on:

ROAS

CAC

CVR

CM2

4. Busupporting a Two Lane Marketing & Evaluation System

Right now, everything in marketing is being judged by one yardstick: ROAS. That forces all money, messaging, and creativity into bottom-funnel selling and that’s exactly why:

Demand never grows

Creative burns out

Scaling breaks unit economics

Growth keeps stalling

The two-lane system is designed to separate two very different jobs that marketing has so each can win on its own terms.



Lane 2: Convert People who already have intent.

This lane is about:

Helping people choose

Making products clear

Removing friction

Closing the sale

What runs here

Lower MOFU

BOFU

PDPs

Retargeting with product

Testimonials

Offers, bundles, routing

How success is judged: Hard numbers only

ROAS

CAC

CVR

CM2

If this lane underperforms: we fix or kill fast. No sentimentality here.

The critical shift in thinking

Before:

Every rupee must perform like a salesperson.

Now:

Some rupees are teachers. Some rupees are closers.

And we stop judging teachers by closing metrics.

With this system:

Lane 1 busupports future demand

Lane 2 harvests current demand

And they stop cannibalising each other.

We’ve separated marketing into two lanes, one that busupports belief and future demand, and one that converts today’s intent, and we will never judge them by the same metrics again.

13. WHAT THIS MEANS FOR OUR TEAM

Brand & Copy

Stop feature-first writing

Start diagnosis-first storytelling

Busupport repeatable language, not campaign slogans

Social

Prioritise belief clarity over trends

Treat humour as support, not centre

Busupporting an authority voice 

Influencer

Brief creators on belief, not product USPs

Evaluate on resonance, not just views

Ads

Separate lanes structurally

Stop judging TOFU like BOFU

Protect the system from ROAS panic

Finally,

This is not a campaign.
This is not a quarter plan.
This is an overall brand reset.

90 Day Plan: Creative Team

Why? Because Creators only work if the rest of the system is ready.

Creators do not carry the full marketing load. Our creative team is responsible for locking attribution and conversion.

A. MID FUNNEL CREATIVE BRIEF (EDUCATION & CLARITY)

Objective

Explain why recovery during sleep matters

Busupport confidence in SleepyCat's logic

Prepare users for decision

Channels

Meta (video & statics)

YouTube

Website guides

SEO

Creative Directions

"Why back pain shows up in the morning"

"Firm vs supportive — why they're not the same"

"What recovery during sleep actually means"

KPIs

Time spent

CTR to guides

Retargeting pool quality

CPM efficiency

B. BOTTOM FUNNEL CREATIVE BRIEF (PROOF & DECISION)

Objective

Remove fear of choosing wrong

Justify premium

Convert high-intent users

Channels

Retargeting ads

PDP sections

Search capture

Creative Directions

"What makes a mattress genuinely supportive for recovery"

"Who this mattress is for / not for"

"Why SleepyCat designs for recovery, not firmness"

KPIs

Conversion rate

Assisted ROAS

Reduced returns / exchanges

DAYS 1–30: FOUNDATION & ALIGNMENT

PDP CHANGES (MANDATORY)
Each key mattress PDP must include:

"Who this is for / not for" (explicit)

Recovery-first explanation (before specs)

Reduced "ortho" prominence (descriptor only)


SEO & FAQ BUsupport
Create problem-first pages:

"Why back pain is worse in the morning"

"Does the mattress affect recovery?"

"Firm vs supportive mattress"

These pages:

Capture post-creator search demand 

Act as mid-funnel education assets

Owner: SEO / Content

Deadline: End of Day 30

A. EDUCATION ADS (CREATIVE TEAM)


Formats:

20–30 sec explainers (Founder led videos, educational authority videos etc)

Static belief cards

Messages:

Recovery vs firmness

Why training isn't the issue

How pressure affects sleep

KPIs:

CTR vs baseline

Time on site

Retargeting pool quality


B. RETARGETING SEQUENCING

Anyone who:

Watches creator content (TOFU Ads)

Visits belief pages -

Must see:

Founder-led explainers

Brand logic ads

This is where attribution locks in.

BOTTOM FUNNEL OPTIMISATION

A. DECISION & PROOF ADS

Messages:

"Who this mattress is for / not for"

"Why SleepyCat designs for recovery"

Controlled ortho descriptors (contextual only)

KPIs:

Conversion rate

Assisted ROAS

Reduced returns

B. SEARCH CAPTURE

Aggressive capture of:

Brand + back pain

Brand + recovery

Defensive ortho queries







Day

Milestone

0

Alignment & approvals

15

First belief signals reviewed

30

PDP + SEO live

45

Consolidation decision

60

Attribution check

90

Demand engine evaluation



6. ALIGNMENTS REQUIRED (CRITICAL)

This plan requires alignment across:

Brand / Content (belief & creators)

Creative team: create clarity and conversion intent

Performance (buying, sequencing)

Growth / Product (PDPs)

SEO (capture)

Retail (language discipline)

If any one breaks, the system weakens. If we do it right, demand compounds.

New Brand Guidelines

Old Identity
New Identity
Sleepycat X Thought Over Design
Real comfort begins with a total relaxation of the body, mind & spirit.

Being relaxed at all times is an essential ingredient to life.
The insight that drives the brand strategy:
People getting comfortable in their own skin & believe in a balanced lifestyle.
The audience : 
“Comfort” as a term is commonplace, so it’s the biggest creative challenge to bring out the nuance.


The challenge : 
PURPOSE:To make guilt-free rest, an equal priority.
STRONGEST BENEFIT:
Learn the art of rest. 

PERSONALITY: Confident, witty and chilled out.
CULTURE: 
We’re intuitive in development, youthful in thought, and positive in influencing a mindset change. 




RTBS:
Sleep expertise
Innovative designs
Curated collection of simple solutions
Premium materials 
End to end quality control through in-house production

BELIEFS / VALUES:To always create well-designed, high quality products that simplify rest.
METHOD: 
By exemplifying the culture of rest, which helps you experience a richer life.
PROOF:“I love resting as much as I love being awake”

PROBLEM: People view bedrooms as an area of life that doesn't ‘deserve' investment. 
OPPORTUNITY: To take beds, bedrooms and comfort beyond “obligatory sleep” and prioritise “rest” as a conscious & desirable action, everyday.


CULTURAL INSIGHT: Indians believe that if you're not constantly hustling, you're failing. 
Rest is for the LAZY.


SLEEPYCAT BRAND KEY

To make comfort, self-care and relaxation guilt-free & an EQUAL PRIORITY					
				
			
		


			
Pave the way to CHANGE the way Indians look at rest

These are the spaces we want to be in

Indian brands in the PURPLE space
Let SleepyCat show you how to embrace “rest for balance”
Show the contrast, by balancing the “heat” of the outward by “cool” of inward

Exaggerate the need for rest → Debunk the myth of “rest is for the lazy”
